review:
Tem jogo que você joga pra se divertir.
Tem jogo que você joga pra competir.
E tem o Euro Truck Simulator 2… que você abre às 22h achando que vai fazer uma entrega rapidinha de batata congelada pra Alemanha e, quando percebe, já são 4h17 da manhã e você tá brigando mentalmente com um pedágio na Hungria como se fosse um caminhoneiro autônomo devendo parcela do Scania.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 não é um jogo.
É um casamento.
Você não “zera” ele. Você assume financiamento.

A proposta é simples: dirigir caminhão pela Europa transportando carga.
Mas a experiência verdadeira vai muito além disso. O jogo lentamente transforma qualquer ser humano normal num tiozão do asfalto que começa a falar frases como:

“Rapaz… esse torque em baixa aqui tá uma delicinha.”
E o pior: você fala isso sinceramente.

No começo você dirige um caminhão de firma com motor cansado que sofre mais que estagiário. O bicho demora 14 quilômetros pra chegar nos 80 km/h e qualquer subida já faz você rezar mais que passageiro de Uno Mille na MS-163.

Mas aí começa a degradação psicológica...

Você faz uma entrega.
Depois outra.
Aí financia seu primeiro caminhão.
Depois coloca um farolzinho.
Um adesivinho.
Uma buzina cromada.
Um painel de LED escrito “Guerreiro da Estrada”.
E pronto. Acabou sua vida social.

Você entra num nível de imersão tão absurdo que começa a respeitar limite de velocidade MESMO SEM TER POLÍCIA NO JOGO. Porque multa de 200 euros dói na alma. O cidadão tá ali, sozinho, 3 da manhã, pista vazia, mas reduz pra 50 km/h numa cidadezinha da Polônia porque “tem radar aqui”.

Isso não é jogar.
Isso é ser PJ ou um CLT se você não tem caminhão.

E o mais impressionante é que o jogo consegue transformar tarefas extremamente comuns em eventos épicos.
Dar ré numa doca vira uma operação militar da OTAN.
Você passa 18 minutos tentando alinhar um reboque enquanto faz movimentos mínimos no volante igual um neurocirurgião tentando separar duas veias. Quando finalmente encaixa perfeitamente, você sente um orgulho que faculdade nenhuma proporciona.

Outro ponto importante: a comunidade desse jogo é composta basicamente por dois tipos de pessoas:
caminhoneiros reais;
adolescentes de 16 anos agindo como caminhoneiros divorciados de 48.
Não existe meio-termo.

E os mods… MEU DEUS os mods.

Você instala um mod achando que vai melhorar os gráficos.
Quando percebe, o jogo virou um carnaval rodoviário.
Tem Scania com luz neon roxa, buzina tocando “Evidências”, painel com anime, caminhão rebaixado e um GPS falando com sotaque gaúcho.

O Euro Truck Simulator 2 respeita tanto a cultura caminhoneira que em certos momentos você consegue SENTIR o cheiro de café de posto e salgado vencido vindo da tela. E aí vem o momento mais perigoso de todos:
quando você compra um volante.

ACABOU.

A partir daí o cidadão não joga mais sentado. Ele PILOTA.
Ele dá seta dentro de casa.
Ele olha no retrovisor imaginário pra trocar de faixa no trânsito real.
Ele começa a avaliar caminhões na rua dizendo: “Esse FH aí tá judiado hein…”

Você não vê mais os seus pais.
Os amigos somem.
Mas a carga de produtos químicos precisa chegar na Bélgica até terça-feira às 14h e isso é o que realmente importa.

Graficamente, o jogo envelheceu igual caminhoneiro raiz: cheio de marcas do tempo, mas ainda firme. E sinceramente? Poucos jogos conseguem criar uma atmosfera tão relaxante. Existe algo terapeuticamente poderoso em dirigir durante a madrugada ouvindo rádio enquanto chove no para-brisa virtual.
É praticamente um spa para homens cansados da vida moderna.

A trilha sonora oficial quase não existe, porque a verdadeira trilha sonora do jogo é:
rádio sertanejo;
forró de caminhoneiro;
podcasts automotivos;
e o pensamento intrusivo de fazer um empréstimo pra comprar um Volvo FH no jogo.
E o mais absurdo é perceber que você cria apego emocional ao caminhão.
Você bate o caminhão e sente culpa.
Você pinta o caminhão.
Você tira print do caminhão no pôr do sol.
Você personaliza o banco como se estivesse decorando apartamento financiado.
Tem gente que cuida menos do próprio carro na vida real.

No fim das contas, Euro Truck Simulator 2 é uma obra-prima da simplicidade.
Ele pega a atividade menos glamourosa do planeta  (entregar carga) e transforma numa jornada quase espiritual.

Você entra querendo brincar de caminhão.
Sai entendendo logística internacional, respeitando caminhoneiro e cogitando seriamente comprar um boné da Scania.

Nota final:

Diversão: 10/10
Realismo: 10/10
Capacidade de destruir sua noção de tempo: 15/10
Chance de você começar a falar “treminhão” sem ironia: inevitável
Risco de virar tiozão: crítico

Recomendado para:

pessoas cansadas da vida;
fãs de estrada;
apreciadores de posto Petrobras, Ipiranga e Taurus;
homens que dizem “esse motor tem presença”;
e qualquer indivíduo que queira descobrir que fazer entrega de pepino da Áustria pra França pode ser estranhamente emocionante.
This game saved me on lonely nights, which are pretty much all of them

Игра очень классная. Большой открытый мир и много модов. Но у игры есть 3 больших недостатка. Первое нету бабушек из-за чего их нельзя сбивать. И второе нету ночных бабочек и иногда бывает скучно из-за этого. И 3 самый главный нету груза 200 и 300 , но есть такой мод. хотелось бы это видеть без мода

Ein zeitloser Klassiker, der immer besser wird
Nach unzähligen Stunden auf den virtuellen Straßen Europas ist es an der Zeit, einmal Danke zu sagen. Der Euro Truck Simulator 2 ist für mich weit mehr als nur ein Spiel; es ist die perfekte Mischung aus Entspannung, Strategie und Technik-Faszination. 

Was ich besonders schätze:
Vorbildliche Weiterentwicklung: Es ist absolut klasse zu sehen, wie ein "altes" Spiel über Jahre hinweg nicht einfach nur am Leben erhalten, sondern konsequent verbessert wird. Ob Grafik-Updates, Sound-Überarbeitungen oder neue Mechaniken – man merkt, dass hier echtes Herzblut fließt.

Stetiger Content-Nachschub: Dass regelmäßig neuer Content dazukommt, hält das Erlebnis frisch. Jedes neue Map-DLC fühlt sich wie eine kleine Entdeckungsreise an, die den hohen Qualitätsstandard hält. 

Die Atmosphäre: Es gibt kaum etwas Beruhigenderes, als bei Regen durch die Alpen zu fahren oder den Sonnenaufgang auf einer Autobahn in Skandinavien zu erleben. 

Ein Wunsch für die Zukunft:
Was mir persönlich wirklich am Herzen liegt: Ich würde mich riesig freuen, wenn die Reise irgendwann auch nach Russland weitergeht. Es ist eine wunderschöne, weite Landschaft, die fahrerisch unglaublich viel Potenzial bietet. Ich fände es richtig, das Politikum hier mal außer Acht zu lassen – am Ende geht es um die Liebe zur Geografie, zum Fahren und zum Entdecken dieser riesigen Welt. 

Fazit:
Wer eine Simulation sucht, die Tiefe besitzt und trotzdem zum Abschalten einlädt, kommt an ETS2 nicht vorbei. Die Entwicklung der letzten Jahre ist beeindruckend und ich bin gespannt, wohin die Reise noch führt. Absolute Empfehlung! 🔥
Gracias a este juego y al señor Toño, mi profesor de autoescuela tengo el carnet de conducir. El no lo sabe pero la noche anterior al examen me hice un bilbao - almeria llevando tomates

Game has no right being this comfy and addictive.
If you like road trips, game can't be beat.
太好玩了，简直是失眠患者的福音。只要睡不着就来开一把欧卡，一两百公里就想睡觉了，堪比安眠药👍👍👍

10/10 Można wozić chlor i amunicję z Niemiec do Polski. Niestety jest za mało imigrantów próbujących wskoczyć na naczepę w Calais oraz francuskie parkingi są zbyt bezpieczne do nocowania względem stanu faktycznego bo brakuje Abduli próbujących cię okraść.

这是一款非常消遣、非常休闲的游戏。
有些游戏会让你被它的画面和玩法惊艳，有些游戏会让你被它的剧情和设计震撼。但《欧洲卡车模拟器》很特殊，它几乎没有任何一个瞬间会让玩家大喊“神作”，也没有什么特别强烈的视觉冲击、剧情爆点或者玩法创新。可它就是能让人不知不觉沉浸进去。
你坐在电脑前，扮演一个现实里带着几分神秘感的大车司机。夜晚的公路、仪表盘的微光、发动机的低鸣、车窗外缓慢后退的城市和山路，再配上一首歌，或者干脆什么都不听，就这么开着。不是在挑战难度，也不是在刷装备，而是在一种近乎放空的节奏里，把现实生活的杂音暂时关掉。
《欧洲卡车模拟器》最厉害的地方，不是它有多刺激，而是它足够不刺激。它给玩家的不是肾上腺素，而是一种稳定、缓慢、可控的松弛感。吊着烟，听着音乐，开着长途，脑子里什么都不用想，只需要看路、换道、控制车速，把货安全送到目的地。
它不惊艳，但它安静；它不热血，但它治愈；它不像一场冒险，更像是一场放空自己的电子冥想。

Лучший симулятор психолога. Самая медитативная игра, ничего не требующая от игрока. От казуала до полноценного сима здесь можно настроить всё. Ты можешь зайти сюда с рулём поиграть с кентами и пообщаться или посидеть соло на клаве ночью в час личной тишины под любимые успокаивающие треки. Ничто так не расслабляет, как рейс на полтора-два часа реального времени и возможность спокойно обсудить жизнь с друзьями или с самим собой.

Хорошая игра, расслабляет до момента когда нужно чинить свою фуру..
400.000 евро кредита делает больно, не берите кредиты!
Great game, run without problems max graphics.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 não é apenas meu jogo mais jogado da Steam. É provavelmente o jogo que melhor representa o tipo de experiência que eu realmente gosto: liberdade, imersão, progressão e estrada.

O ETS2 tem algo raro hoje em dia. Ele não depende de ação frenética ou recompensa instantânea para ser marcante. O jogo simplesmente te coloca na cabine, entrega uma rota e deixa a viagem acontecer. E de alguma forma isso funciona absurdamente bem.

O mais impressionante é como a SCS conseguiu transformar direção em uma experiência quase terapêutica. Existem momentos em que você entra no jogo apenas para relaxar, ouvir o som do motor, pegar uma estrada longa atravessando países e esquecer completamente do tempo passando.

E quanto mais você joga, mais percebe o cuidado nos detalhes:

cada país tem identidade própria;
as estradas mudam completamente de clima e estilo;
as cidades passam sensações diferentes;
as DLCs realmente expandem o mundo ao invés de parecer apenas conteúdo reciclado.

Meu perfil no ETS2 mostra exatamente isso. Não foi uma jornada corrida tentando “fechar jogo”. Foi uma construção natural ao longo do tempo. Explorando regiões, encarando cargas especiais, dominando manobras difíceis, fazendo entregas perfeitas e evoluindo junto com o próprio simulador.

Uma das coisas que mais gosto no ETS2 é que ele recompensa direção limpa e paciência. Fazer entregas longas sem dano, controlar cargas gigantes em estradas estreitas, estacionar corretamente e concluir rotas difíceis traz uma satisfação genuína que poucos jogos conseguem entregar hoje.

E existe outro ponto forte: o jogo continua evoluindo sem perder sua essência. Cada atualização melhora gráficos, iluminação, física, sons e mapas, mas aquela sensação clássica de pegar a estrada à noite continua intacta. ETS2 ainda tem aquela atmosfera única que nenhum outro simulador conseguiu copiar direito.

As DLCs também elevam o jogo para outro nível. Viajar pelos Bálcãs, Escandinávia, França, Itália, Mar Negro, Grécia ou regiões nórdicas faz o mapa parecer uma viagem real pela Europa. Você sente diferença cultural, visual e até no ritmo das estradas.

No fim, Euro Truck Simulator 2 virou muito mais do que um simulador de caminhão para mim. Virou um jogo para apreciar a viagem. Um lugar para relaxar, explorar, evoluir e simplesmente curtir a estrada no próprio ritmo.

E honestamente? Pouquíssimos jogos conseguem permanecer tão bons por tanto tempo sem perder a alma.

Juego come horas, si no dispones de horas suficientes para jugar evitalo, se te pasan las horas sin darte cuenta, y mas si tienes la posibilidad de jugar con volante (recomendable para disfrutar de la experiencia a otro nivel) y de poder jugar convoy con tus amigos, gran simulador de conduccion y de vida camionera. Buen pasatiempo!

Euro Truck Simulator 2 – 70+ Stunden später

Auch wenn das Spiel sich inzwischen anfühlt, als wäre es schon 20 Jahre alt, hat ETS2 für mich immer noch etwas Besonderes. Mir gefällt das Spiel wirklich, aber ich spiele es trotzdem eher selten. Es ist nichts, was ich täglich starte, eher ein Titel, den ich anwerfe, wenn ich einfach mal abschalten will.

Die Atmosphäre ist entspannt, die Fahrten haben etwas Beruhigendes und man verliert schnell das Zeitgefühl, wenn man gemütlich über die Autobahn rollt. Die Trucks fühlen sich gut an, die Welt ist groß genug und man merkt, wie viel Liebe im Detail steckt, auch wenn die Grafik nicht mehr die neueste ist.

Klar, die KI baut manchmal Unsinn und manche Strecken kennt man irgendwann auswendig. Aber irgendwie gehört das dazu. ETS2 ist für mich ein Spiel, das man immer wieder mal auspackt, ohne dass es seinen Charme verliert.

Für alle, die entspannte Spiele mögen, definitiv eine Empfehlung, auch wenn man es nicht jeden Tag spielt.

『Euro Truck Simulator 2（ETS2）』が発売から10年以上経っても愛され続けている理由は、これが単なる「運転ゲーム」ではなく、極めて精巧な「空間と時間のシミュレーター」だからでしょう。
実車でハイエースや2トントラックを転がした経験がありますが、似たような経験がある方でもそうでなくても、このゲームが持つ「説得力」にすぐ気づくはずです。

■ 2トン車とは異なる「質量」のリアリティ
仕事でハンドルを握る人にとって、車両のサイズ感や挙動は感覚に染み付いているものです。ETS2で扱う大型トラックは、それらとは全く異なる「巨大な慣性」を持っています。
特に中古でもハンコン（ステアリングコントローラー）を導入すると、その体験は一気に深まります。路面のギャップや、カーブでの荷重移動が手に伝わってくる感覚。40トン近い荷を引いて坂道を登る際、エンジンの回転数を確認しながら慎重にギアを選ぶ作業は、機械を操る根源的な楽しさに満ちています。

■ 「一人の運転手」から「経営者」へ
本作の魅力は、道路の上だけではありません。
最初はフリーの運転手として食いつなぎ、貯めた資金で最初の一台を購入し、やがてガレージを構えて従業員を雇う。このビジネスの成長プロセスが、非常に心地よいテンポで進んでいきます。
自分のオフィス（運転席）を自分好みにカスタマイズし、少しずつ会社を大きくしていく感覚は、日々着実に物事を進めることを好む大人にとって、最高の達成感を与えてくれるはずです。

■ 欧州の空気感を走る
ベースマップだけでも、その風景の移り変わりは十分に楽しめます。
日本の道路とは異なる信号の配置、独特なラウンドアバウト、そして広大な田園風景。Farming SimulatorやCar Mechanic Simulatorのような「没入型シミュレーション」を好む層にとっても、このディテールへのこだわりは納得のいくクオリティでしょう。
何も考えず、夜のアウトバーンを流す時間は、現代人にとって貴重な「デジタル・デトックス」の時間にもなり得ます。

■ 総評
まずは基本セットで、大型車両を御する手応えと、会社経営の醍醐味をじっくり味わってみてください。セール時にマップDLCを買い足していけば、この旅はさらにどこまでも広がっていきます。
派手な演出はありませんが、一度ハンドルを握れば、数時間はあっという間に過ぎ去ってしまう。そんな「静かな中毒性」を秘めた名作です。

However there is one thing i despise about it, and that is the new UI, the old one held all the info we needed/wanted and we could swap between info pages using the function keys on the keyboard. They changed the UI to the simplified slop to cater to console players

Absolutely expand your player base SCS but don't punish pc players by ruining a fully functional UI and forcing hints onto us, make it clear how to disable hints at least

Can we get a setting to change back to the old ui?

Don't mistake me this change makes this new ui a teensy bit more palatable, but i personally still hate it and believe that SCS has more than enough skill to add that to the settings, i mean hell the code and images for the ui already exist

some of us just want the old ui as an option, not a full reversion of the ui

I will still be playing this game though

Ein echt geile Spiel, und es wird von Jahr zu Jahr immer besser. Weiter so:

Euro Truck Simulator 2, c’est pas un jeu.
C’est une thérapie de couple avec la solitude.

C’est le seul titre où tu peux dire sans ironie :

“J’ai passé 3 heures à livrer du yaourt en Pologne et j’ai ressenti quelque chose.”

Le concept est magnifique de simplicité malade :
tu conduis un camion.
Pendant longtemps.
Très longtemps.
Parfois tu tournes.
Parfois tu freines.
Parfois tu existes.

Et c’est vendu comme du fun.

Le gameplay de Euro Truck Simulator 2, c’est :

respecter des limitations de vitesse que personne ne respecte dans la vraie vie,
t’endormir en regardant des autoroutes infinies,
et gagner de l’argent fictif pour acheter un autre camion afin de refaire exactement la même chose mais plus loin.

C’est Capitalisme Simulator avec des pauses clignotant gauche.

Et pourtant les joueurs sont sérieux. Très sérieux.

Ils parlent du jeu comme s’ils géraient une multinationale :

“J’ai optimisé ma route Rotterdam–Prague pour réduire les coûts de carburant.”

Frère.
Tu transportes des palettes de jus de pomme dans un Scania virtuel à 90 km/h.
Tu n’es pas Elon Musk. Tu es un GPS avec un contrat CDI.

Mais le vrai génie du jeu, c’est l’ambiance.

C’est un jeu où :

la pluie tombe toujours au bon moment pour te rendre triste,
la radio diffuse des musiques que même Spotify a oubliées,
et les paysages te font comprendre que l’Europe est juste un long couloir administratif avec des arbres.

Et pourtant… c’est hypnotique.

Parce que ton cerveau finit par accepter une vérité dangereuse :

rouler tout droit pendant 25 minutes, c’est une activité.

Tu commences en mode “je vais juste tester 10 minutes”
et tu te retrouves à dire :

“allez encore une livraison et j’arrête ma vie sociale.”

Les DLC sont une autre blague philosophique.
“Scandinavie”, “Ibérie”, “Going East”…
bientôt :

“Euro Truck Simulator 2 : Parking de supermarché édition premium”

Et malgré tout, Euro Truck Simulator 2 reste dangereux :
pas parce qu’il est stressant…
mais parce qu’il est trop apaisant.

C’est un jeu où tu réalises que ton moment préféré de la journée,
c’est un rond-point en Allemagne à 3h du matin avec du jazz radio en fond.

Note finale :

10/10 en relaxation suspecte
9/10 en monotonie hypnotique
100/10 en “je suis devenu chauffeur routier volontaire”

Wciąga, relaksuje i wciąga jeszcze bardziej. Gra, która od 2012 roku jest nieustannie rozwijana z prawdziwą pasją. Poza nowymi DLC-ami SCS regularnie odnawia starsze mapy, dzięki czemu cała Europa wygląda coraz lepiej.

[b] Плюсы [/b]

- Расслабляющий геймплей
- Отличная симуляция вождения большегрузов
- Огромная карта, техника и визуальные улучшения от сообщества дают практически бесконечный контент

[b] Минусы [/b]

- Со временем поездки могут стать однообразными

- Медленный темп
- Интерфейс и управление экономикой иногда выглядят простовато или малоинформативно для продвинутого менеджмента
- Редкие технические баги.

[b] Вывод [/b]

Tem jogo que você joga pra se divertir.
Tem jogo que você joga pra competir.
E tem o Euro Truck Simulator 2… que você abre às 22h achando que vai fazer uma entrega rapidinha de batata congelada pra Alemanha e, quando percebe, já são 4h17 da manhã e você tá brigando mentalmente com um pedágio na Hungria como se fosse um caminhoneiro autônomo devendo parcela do Scania.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 não é um jogo.
É um casamento.
Você não “zera” ele. Você assume financiamento.

A proposta é simples: dirigir caminhão pela Europa transportando carga.
Mas a experiência verdadeira vai muito além disso. O jogo lentamente transforma qualquer ser humano normal num tiozão do asfalto que começa a falar frases como:

“Rapaz… esse torque em baixa aqui tá uma delicinha.”
E o pior: você fala isso sinceramente.

No começo você dirige um caminhão de firma com motor cansado que sofre mais que estagiário. O bicho demora 14 quilômetros pra chegar nos 80 km/h e qualquer subida já faz você rezar mais que passageiro de Uno Mille na MS-163.

Mas aí começa a degradação psicológica...

Você faz uma entrega.
Depois outra.
Aí financia seu primeiro caminhão.
Depois coloca um farolzinho.
Um adesivinho.
Uma buzina cromada.
Um painel de LED escrito “Guerreiro da Estrada”.
E pronto. Acabou sua vida social.

Você entra num nível de imersão tão absurdo que começa a respeitar limite de velocidade MESMO SEM TER POLÍCIA NO JOGO. Porque multa de 200 euros dói na alma. O cidadão tá ali, sozinho, 3 da manhã, pista vazia, mas reduz pra 50 km/h numa cidadezinha da Polônia porque “tem radar aqui”.

Isso não é jogar.
Isso é ser PJ ou um CLT se você não tem caminhão.

E o mais impressionante é que o jogo consegue transformar tarefas extremamente comuns em eventos épicos.
Dar ré numa doca vira uma operação militar da OTAN.
Você passa 18 minutos tentando alinhar um reboque enquanto faz movimentos mínimos no volante igual um neurocirurgião tentando separar duas veias. Quando finalmente encaixa perfeitamente, você sente um orgulho que faculdade nenhuma proporciona.

Outro ponto importante: a comunidade desse jogo é composta basicamente por dois tipos de pessoas:
caminhoneiros reais;
adolescentes de 16 anos agindo como caminhoneiros divorciados de 48.
Não existe meio-termo.

E os mods… MEU DEUS os mods.

Você instala um mod achando que vai melhorar os gráficos.
Quando percebe, o jogo virou um carnaval rodoviário.
Tem Scania com luz neon roxa, buzina tocando “Evidências”, painel com anime, caminhão rebaixado e um GPS falando com sotaque gaúcho.

O Euro Truck Simulator 2 respeita tanto a cultura caminhoneira que em certos momentos você consegue SENTIR o cheiro de café de posto e salgado vencido vindo da tela. E aí vem o momento mais perigoso de todos:
quando você compra um volante.

ACABOU.

A partir daí o cidadão não joga mais sentado. Ele PILOTA.
Ele dá seta dentro de casa.
Ele olha no retrovisor imaginário pra trocar de faixa no trânsito real.
Ele começa a avaliar caminhões na rua dizendo: “Esse FH aí tá judiado hein…”

Você não vê mais os seus pais.
Os amigos somem.
Mas a carga de produtos químicos precisa chegar na Bélgica até terça-feira às 14h e isso é o que realmente importa.

Graficamente, o jogo envelheceu igual caminhoneiro raiz: cheio de marcas do tempo, mas ainda firme. E sinceramente? Poucos jogos conseguem criar uma atmosfera tão relaxante. Existe algo terapeuticamente poderoso em dirigir durante a madrugada ouvindo rádio enquanto chove no para-brisa virtual.
É praticamente um spa para homens cansados da vida moderna.

A trilha sonora oficial quase não existe, porque a verdadeira trilha sonora do jogo é:
rádio sertanejo;
forró de caminhoneiro;
podcasts automotivos;
e o pensamento intrusivo de fazer um empréstimo pra comprar um Volvo FH no jogo.
E o mais absurdo é perceber que você cria apego emocional ao caminhão.
Você bate o caminhão e sente culpa.
Você pinta o caminhão.
Você tira print do caminhão no pôr do sol.
Você personaliza o banco como se estivesse decorando apartamento financiado.
Tem gente que cuida menos do próprio carro na vida real.

No fim das contas, Euro Truck Simulator 2 é uma obra-prima da simplicidade.
Ele pega a atividade menos glamourosa do planeta (entregar carga) e transforma numa jornada quase espiritual.

Você entra querendo brincar de caminhão.
Sai entendendo logística internacional, respeitando caminhoneiro e cogitando seriamente comprar um boné da Scania.

Nota final:

Diversão: 10/10
Realismo: 10/10
Capacidade de destruir sua noção de tempo: 15/10
Chance de você começar a falar “treminhão” sem ironia: inevitável
Risco de virar tiozão: crítico

Recomendado para:

pessoas cansadas da vida;
fãs de estrada;
apreciadores de posto Petrobras, Ipiranga e Taurus;
homens que dizem “esse motor tem presença”;
e qualquer indivíduo que queira descobrir que fazer entrega de pepino da Áustria pra França pode ser estranhamente emocionante.

Convoy mode is honestly a mess.

Neither my convoy nor my friend's convoy shows up when searching the server browser, even though our download regions are the same and all filters are set correctly. The only workaround is joining through Steam, but that just throws a mod mismatch error.

The worst part is that I can manually send my friend the exact mods and load order and make it work, but I shouldn't have to do that. The game should handle that automatically, the same way it does when joining convoys through the server browser. If the system can detect and sync mods there, it should not completely fall apart when joining through Steam.

On top of that, the server browser itself is horribly optimized. I am running an RTX 3080 and the browser sits at a solid 24 FPS, which is ridiculous for what is basically just a list of servers.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 is a great game in many ways, but Convoy mode feels unreliable, clunky, and unfinished. Multiplayer should not require this much troubleshooting just to play with one friend.

Just stick to TruckersMP if you want to play with a friend, only downside is no traffic and no mods.

Лучший симулятор кирпича с крыльями! А также программа утилизации легковых машин и творога

 (★★★★★, пять колёсных гайок из пяти)

Купил игру, потому что хотел спокойно довезти творог из Калуги во Владивосток. А получил гиперзвуковой боевой кирпич на колёсах, который физика даже не подозревает! Моя фура ведёт себя как кирпич с  аэроидинахуй -на спидометре 165 км/ч, и тут из-за поворота выезжает малюсенькая легковушка.

Я даже не успел моргнуть. Мы встретились лоб в лоб. И знаете что? Легковушка сложилась в гармошку, а на моём бампере даже царапины не осталось! Игра честно написала: «Вы нанесли урона: 0.01%». Теперь я возглавляю Клуб анонимных разрушителей микролитражек — за неделю я отправил на свалку 40 «Жигулей» и один трамвай (трамвай пытался объехать, но у него тоже не вышло).

Особый восторг — режим конвоя. Вы можете пригласить друга, сесть в две фуры и… отправить его покорять космические пространства! Достаточно на скорости 165 въехать в его прицеп сзади. Друг вылетает за текстуры, делает орбитальный облёт спутника Земли и приземляется через 10 минут где-нибудь в футуристическом DLC «Марс-2069». Разработчики, спасибо за бесплатный билет на Falcon 9! Только друзей быстро не остаётся — все в открытом космосе.

Но главный кайф — перевозки творога. Я взял заказ: «Нежный творог, 20 тонн, из Рязани в Магадан, срок: вчера». Я погнал, выжимая 165 на каждой кочке. Влетел в отбойник, перевернулся, собрался, снова влетел. Приезжаю в Магадан. Открываю прицеп. Творога там больше нет — зато есть уникальный творожно-металлический сплав! Лопасти вентилятора, кусок моей оси, левая фара и радостная хрюшка (откуда?). Клиент сказал: «Это лучший творог в моей жизни, он хрустит и искрит!» Теперь я вожу не творог, а набор для сборки автомобиля с творожным вкусом. Каждая партия уникальна — сегодня там болты, завтра подушка двигателя.

А недавно я вёз жидкий азот в открытой цистерне через всю Европу с остановками на шашлыки. Азот выкипел на первой заправке, но я вместо него залил борщ и доставил. Клиент расплакался от счастья: «Это лучший тёплый борщ с пузырьками!» Игра дарит свободу творчества: вы можете перевозить что угодно, как угодно и куда угодно. Главное — скорость выше 160, а остальное приложится.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 é aquele tipo de jogo que parece simples olhando de fora, mas quando você começa a jogar percebe o nível de imersão que ele entrega. A sensação de pegar estrada, fazer entregas longas e montar sua própria empresa de transportes acaba sendo muito mais viciante do que parece.

Os gráficos continuam bonitos, principalmente com mods e DLCs, e a ambientação deixa tudo muito realista. Dirigir durante a noite, na chuva ou atravessando diferentes países da Europa passa uma sensação muito boa de liberdade e tranquilidade.

Outro ponto forte é a progressão. Você começa fazendo pequenos fretes e, com o tempo, consegue comprar caminhões melhores, expandir garagens e contratar motoristas. Isso dá uma sensação constante de evolução.

O jogo pode ficar repetitivo para quem procura ação o tempo todo, mas para quem gosta de simuladores e jogos mais calmos, ele é excelente. A comunidade também ajuda muito, trazendo mods incríveis que aumentam ainda mais a experiência.

No geral, Euro Truck Simulator 2 é um dos melhores simuladores já feitos e continua valendo muito a pena mesmo depois de tantos anos.
отличная игра, особенно после тяжелого дня. Под спокойную музыку можно хорошо раслабиться, катаясь по дорогам европейских стран

Прекрасная игра. Особенно с модификациями на русские дороги и тягачи. Везёшь груз на несколько тысяч километров, а за окном твоей фуры темно. Только лишь фары освещают твою дорогу, а в салоне красиво горит тёплым светом приборная панель. Впрочем, игровым днём тоже ездить хорошо - можно посмотреть на природные красоты, но ночь в этой игре это нечто прекрасное.

600 hours in and I can confidently say that ETS2 has completely ruined normal driving for me.
Now every time I see a roundabout IRL, I instinctively check my mirrors and look for a trailer that isn’t there.

This game somehow made delivering virtual potatoes across Europe more exciting than most AAA action games. I started playing “just for a quick drive” and suddenly it was 3AM, I had 17 speeding tickets, and I was emotionally attached to my truck.

The graphics are relaxing, the driving feels amazing, and nothing beats the feeling of perfectly parking a 20-ton trailer on the first try after failing 14 times in a row. Pure happiness.

Also shoutout to ETS2 for teaching me more European geography than school ever did. I now know 47 different highways and exactly where I crashed into a guardrail in every country.

10/10 game.
Would absolutely spend another 600 hours transporting yogurt across Germany.

steamのワークショップでナビMODを導入する事を強くオススメします。
このゲームはナビが音声無しというデフォルトなのですが非常にナビが間違いやすくわたしは何度も道を間違いました。

これを導入すると世界が変わります。
リアルに限りなく近付くんです。

例えば高速道路での３車線道路が急に２車線になるから車線変更しておいた方がいいのにそれがわからず、高速道路から降りる羽目になるというのがダントツに多かったんです。
この欠点をナビが埋めてくれます。
非常にわかりやすく教えてくれるようになります。

开着车听着歌，看着风景，仿佛时间都在这一刻慢了下来。在欧卡2里，没有PVP游戏中那种时刻紧绷神经的竞争与浮躁，不必在意队友的谩骂或对手的超越，你的任务纯粹而宁静——只是启动引擎，挂上档位，将货物安全地运送到远方。这种极致的安宁感与自由感，只需要你享受这段“说走就走”的旅行，在日复一日的平稳驾驶中，找到那份久违的惬意与内心的平静。

Mi último acto de amor fue dejarla ir, y tal vez fue lo mejor que pude hacer por que ella conoció a alguien más y la verdad es que se ve más feliz con el que como lo era conmigo y eso me alegra bastante verla feliz con alguien más pero en el fondo ese alguien me hubiera gustado ser yo, no la recuperé por la misma razon de que ella ya no era feliz conmigo y lo intenté varias veces pero al final acabó como un “usuario de Instagram” y ahora solo quedan los recuerdos las veces que era feliz con ella cuando no importaba el clima, la hora, el lugar o con quienes solo quería verla. Nuestra relación no duró bastante pero antes de eso sentí esa verdadera chispa que sientes cuando encuentras a tu verdadero amor, nuestra conexión era especial nos entendíamos perfectamente me sentía cómodo con ella, hablar todas las noches y reírnos de nosotros mismos esperarla en la puerta de nuestra academia solo para darle un abrazo, acompañarla hasta su casa y ver de qué llegue sana y salva y yo me regresaba a mi casa caminando bajo el sol, bajo la lluvia o un día nublado pero siempre con una sonrisa de felicidad de haber estado un momento con ella me sentía querido una sensación que no siempre la puedes experimentar. Casi no hubo peleas entre yo y ella nos llevábamos bien sin rencor ni resentimiento terminamos con lo nuestro ella dio el primer mensaje de acabar lo que por un tiempo fueron los mejores días de mi vida, esa tarde mientras regresaba de mi colegio estaba listo para sorprenderla con un ramo de flores pero al escribirle vi su mensaje y entendí que ella tal vez no sentía tanto amor como yo lo sentía por ella, me dijo para ser amigos pero me di cuenta que ya nunca iba a ser igual lo nuestro llegó a su fin y no lo quería creer las primeras semanas me alejé de todo círculo social que esté asociada a ella solo me quedé con unos cuantos amigos luego a los siguientes meses perdí conexión total con ella intenté recuperarla pero me dijo que estábamos bien como amigos y un tiempo una amiga me dijo que se besó alguien más y que eran felices y al principio pensé en volverme la mejor persona para que ella de arrepienta pero luego pensé que si lo logro iba quedar más

In the year of 2026 the textures (=grapics) aren't presentable anymore. The game gets updated regularly, but sadly those new features mostly benefit the arcade side of the game. Train Sim World did a far better job mostly in terms of graphics and changed from railworks engine to Unreal engine 4.
Features to simulate real driving parameters, freight and freight security, specific loading procedures for each freight and different regulations and forms for specific freights don't get presented at all in this game. For comparison, Train Sim World offers a few "Expert Locomotives" which come very close to having all features of the real locomotive. TSW is a good example of a new game which deserves being called a Simulator.

Every Update introduces something Gamplay related or QOL features, UI is not one of these things.
The UI went from charming high quality icons that were easely understandable and recognizable to boring sterile gray icons with no immediate features/colors that set them apart from eachother making it hard to navigate the Menu. This is why the game sadly deserves the Negativ Review for me at this time since its something you interact with all the time.

Positiv:
ETS 2 bietet eine großartige Community, eine hervorragende Mod-Unterstützung und wird von den Entwicklern seit Jahren kontinuierlich weiterentwickelt. Neue Karten, technische Verbesserungen und Grafik-Updates halten das Spiel aktuell.

Negativ:
Für eine Simulation fehlt es dem Spiel jedoch an Tiefe. Ohne Mods beschränkt sich die Fahrzeugauswahl größtenteils auf Sattelzugmaschinen mit Auflieger. Fahrzeugtypen wie 7,5-Tonner, Tandem- oder Hängerzüge fehlen. Auch die Logistik ist sehr einfach gehalten: Man fährt überwiegend von A nach B, während Funktionen wie Sammelguttransporte, Mehrstopp-Touren oder die Beladung verschiedener Waren für mehrere Kunden nicht vorhanden sind..

Fazit:
Das Fahren selbst macht Spaß und ist gut umgesetzt. Allerdings fehlt es dem Spiel in den Bereichen Logistik, Fuhrparkvielfalt und Unternehmensmanagement an Tiefe. Für mich reicht es deshalb nicht für eine positive Bewertung. 👎

Игра отличная. Не смотря на год выпуска до сих пор  развивается. Отличная графика, огромное количество обновлений и дополнений. Стала очень разнообразной и интересной. Настойчиво рекомендую любителям этого жанра.

Maybe it's my old age or the weariness of life itself, I don't know and I don't care. I have no scientific explanation for why I find myself returning to this game, but Euro Truck Simulator 2 is the best thing to end the day with, except for my wife of course. driving a truck and that's it. That's the whole story.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 is one of the few games that successfully turns having a job into entertainment.

On paper, it sounds incredibly boring. You drive a truck. You pick up cargo. You deliver cargo. Then you do it again. Somehow, against all logic and common sense, it's one of the most relaxing and enjoyable games I've ever played.

The graphics are great, the map is massive, and there's something oddly satisfying about spending hours driving across Europe while obeying traffic laws more carefully than I do in most racing games. Nothing gets the adrenaline pumping quite like using your turn signal correctly and successfully backing a trailer into a loading dock on the first attempt.

The progression is surprisingly addictive. You start out taking jobs for other companies, scraping together enough money to buy your first truck. Before you know it, you're managing a fleet, hiring drivers, expanding garages, and checking your company's finances like you're the CEO of a multinational trucking empire. All because you delivered a few pallets of frozen fish on time.

The attention to detail is impressive. Weather, traffic, customization, and the variety of roads make every trip feel different. Even after hundreds of hours, I still find myself saying, "Just one more delivery," before realizing it's somehow 3 a.m. and I've spent the evening transporting industrial machinery across three countries.

Of course, the game isn't perfect. Sometimes the AI drivers seem to have obtained their licenses from the back of a cereal box, and GPS directions can occasionally inspire more confidence than accuracy. But honestly, those moments just add character to the experience.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 proves that you don't need explosions, zombies, or world-ending threats to make a great game. Sometimes all you need is a truck, a destination, and enough patience to drive 500 miles without clipping a guardrail.

10/10.

I bought it expecting to drive a truck for a few hours. Instead, I accidentally started a logistics company and now I get excited about fuel prices and trailer configurations. I have become the very thing I never thought I'd be: a virtual truck driver who complains about traffic while voluntarily sitting in more traffic.

After hundreds of hours, I still find myself coming back to ETS2. The map is huge, the trucks feel great to drive, and the constant updates keep the game fresh. Whether you want a relaxing drive after a long day or enjoy building your own trucking company, this game delivers. Definitely one of the best simulation games on Steam.

What an unbelievably miserable piece of game design.

I bought Euro Truck Simulator 2 to drive, relax, and enjoy myself — not to spend an hour trapped in a braindead tutorial trying to reverse-park a trailer with instructions that explain practically nothing.

This pathetic excuse for a tutorial does not teach steering, trailer behavior, positioning, correction, or timing. It simply dumps a new player into a frustrating task and expects them to already know how to drive a truck like some unemployed basement-dwelling veteran with 5,000 hours in the game.

And of course, the hardcore fanboys will defend it with the usual garbage: “It’s easy,” “skill issue,” “just practise.” Congratulations. You mastered an awkward parking mechanic in a truck simulator. Your parents must be incredibly proud.

A tutorial is supposed to teach. This one wastes time, frustrates beginners, and makes the game feel hostile before the actual game has even started. There should be a skip button, a simplified parking option, proper visual guidance, or at least instructions written by someone who understands what the word “tutorial” means.

This is not difficulty. This is not realism. This is lazy, incompetent design protected by obsessive players who confuse wasting hundreds of hours with having talent.

The physics in this game make it feel truly authentic. Unlike most other simulator titles that focus mainly on flashy graphics or eye‑catching gimmicks, SCS prioritizes delivering a genuine simulation experience grounded in real‑world physics. The better your PC or system, the more realistic and immersive the visuals become, enhancing the overall gameplay. I’ve been thoroughly enjoying the experience so far. That said, it would be fantastic if SCS expanded its focus to include Asian countries and roads—especially places like India—where the unpredictability of traffic, diverse terrains, and unique road conditions could add a whole new layer of realism and exploration.

Bazı oyunlar vardır, amacı eğlendirmektir. Bazıları ise sizi bambaşka bir dünyanın içine çeker. Euro Truck Simulator 2 tam olarak bunu başarıyor. İlk bakışta "Tır sürme oyunu mu?" diye düşünebilirsiniz. Ben de ilk başladığımda aynı şeyi düşünmüştüm. Ancak birkaç teslimattan sonra kendimi Avrupa yollarında kilometrelerce yol yaparken buldum.

Oyunda küçük bir nakliye şirketi kurarak kariyerinize başlıyorsunuz. İlk zamanlarda başkalarının araçlarıyla yük taşıyor, para kazandıkça kendi tırınızı satın alıyorsunuz. Zamanla şirketinizi büyütüp yeni sürücüler işe alabiliyor ve kendi lojistik imparatorluğunuzu kurabiliyorsunuz.

ETS2'nin en güçlü yanı atmosferi. Gecenin ilerleyen saatlerinde yağmur altında uzun bir otobanda ilerlerken radyonuzu açıp yolculuk yapmak gerçekten huzur verici. Şehirlerin, otoyolların ve manzaraların detay seviyesi oldukça başarılı. Her teslimat yalnızca bir görev değil, aynı zamanda küçük bir yolculuk deneyimi sunuyor.

Sürüş mekanikleri gerçekçiliği ve oynanabilirliği dengeli bir şekilde sunuyor. Trafik kurallarına uymak, yakıt durumunu kontrol etmek ve uzun yolculukları planlamak oyuna derinlik katıyor. Özellikle direksiyon seti kullanan oyuncular için deneyim çok daha etkileyici hale geliyor.

Mod desteği ise oyunun ömrünü ciddi anlamda uzatıyor. Yeni haritalar, araçlar, grafik paketleri ve çeşitli iyileştirmeler sayesinde yüzlerce saat oynasanız bile sıkılmanız oldukça zor.

Kurzfazit  
Euro Truck Simulator 2 ist mehr als ein Fahrsimulator: es ist eine ruhige, fesselnde Erfahrung, die Geduld belohnt. Das Spiel verbindet entspannendes Cruisen mit überraschend tiefen Management- und Individualisierungsoptionen. Für Fans von Simulationen und Spieler, die gern in eine langsame, detailverliebte Welt eintauchen, ist es ein Must‑Have.

Grafik und Atmosphäre
Die Grafik wirkt trotz ihres Alters beständig stimmig: Landschaften, Städte und Wettereffekte schaffen eine glaubwürdige, oft beruhigende Atmosphäre. Licht und Tageszeiten verleihen langen Strecken eine filmische Qualität, und kleine Details wie Verkehrsschilder, Rastplätze und Industrieanlagen tragen stark zur Immersion bei. Zwar gibt es technisch modernere Titel, doch ETS2 punktet mit einem konsistenten Stil, der die Langstrecke angenehm macht.

Gameplay und Fahrgefühl
Das Fahrgefühl ist zugänglich, aber nuanciert: Einsteiger finden schnell Zugang, während erfahrene Fahrer das Gewicht, die Trägheit und das Bremsverhalten der Lkw zu schätzen wissen. Missionen und Aufträge sind abwechslungsreich genug, um Routine zu vermeiden, und das Balancing zwischen Zeitdruck, Frachtart und Straßenbedingungen sorgt für ständige kleine Entscheidungen. Die KI‑Verkehrsführung ist solide; gelegentliche Ungereimtheiten stören kaum den Spielfluss.

Umfang und Langzeitmotivation
ETS2 bietet enormen Spielumfang: ein weitläufiges Straßennetz, zahlreiche Städte, Fahrzeugmodelle und ein Karriere‑System mit Fuhrparkmanagement. Das Fortschrittssystem motiviert durch neue Aufträge, bessere Trucks und die Möglichkeit, ein eigenes Transportunternehmen aufzubauen. DLCs und Karten‑Erweiterungen erweitern die Welt kontinuierlich und sorgen für Langzeitspaß.

Modding und Community
Ein großer Pluspunkt ist die Modding‑Szene: Community‑Mods bringen neue Karten, Lkw, Sounds und Gameplay‑Erweiterungen, die das Spiel lebendig halten. Die aktive Community liefert regelmäßig frische Inhalte und hilfreiche Tipps, was ETS2 zu einem Spiel macht, das sich über Jahre hinweg weiterentwickelt.

Fazit und Empfehlung
Euro Truck Simulator 2 ist eine seltene Mischung aus entspannendem Roadtrip und befriedigendem Managementspiel. Es belohnt Zeit und Aufmerksamkeit mit einer ruhigen, aber tiefen Spielerfahrung. Wer Geduld für lange Fahrten hat und Freude an Detailarbeit und Individualisierung findet, wird hier viele Stunden sinnvoller Unterhaltung entdecken. Empfehlung: uneingeschränkt für Sim‑Fans und alle, die ein gemächliches, atmosphärisches Spiel suchen.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 es, sin duda, uno de los mejores simuladores de camiones que existen. Es el juego que popularizó el género y, para mucha gente (incluyéndome), sigue siendo el referente.
La fórmula es la misma que en American Truck Simulator, pero ambientada en Europa: te conviertes en camionero y transportas mercancía de un punto a otro por todo el continente. Suena simple, pero la ejecución es brillante.
Lo que hace especial a ETS2 es la variedad brutal del mapa. Con los DLCs de mapas (y hay bastantes: Escandinavia, Francia, Italia, Iberia, Baltic, Black Sea, y los más recientes como Nordic Horizons y Soul of Anatolia), el mundo se siente enorme y vivo. Puedes conducir desde los fiordos de Noruega, cruzar los Alpes, pasar por los túneles de Suiza, recorrer las llanuras de Polonia, atravesar el Canal de la Mancha en ferry o llegar hasta las calles de Estambul. Cada país tiene su propio carácter, su arquitectura, su clima y hasta su forma de conducir. Las rutas nunca se vuelven repetitivas.
9/10 — Un clásico moderno que sigue mejorando con los años. Si te gusta conducir de verdad y sentir el viaje, este juego es una joya.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 — на удивление цепкая игра. Я и сам не ожидал, что она так затянет, а в итоге провёл за рулём гораздо больше времени, чем планировал. Свою цену базовая версия оправдывает честно: её хватает, чтобы понять, заходит тебе такой формат или нет. Тонкость в том, что рано или поздно захочется новых дорог, и вот тут начинается охота за скидками на DLC — если собирать всё и сразу по полной стоимости, бюджет ощутимо трещит. Но даже без дополнений игра даёт достаточно простора.

В одиночку это чистая медитация: ты, трасса, груз и возможность выдохнуть под любимую музыку или подкаст. Атмосфера здесь — главный магнит: дождь на лобовом стекле, утренний туман, смена пейзажей за окном. В компании с друзьями или случайными попутчиками всё превращается в лёгкий хаос: синхронные моргания фарами, перекличка клаксонами и эпичные потери друг друга на развязках. Игра умеет быть и уютным одиночным путешествием, и социальным аттракционом.

Отдельная любовь и боль — искусственный интеллект на дороге. Местные водители будто сдавали на права по купонам, а потом проходили ускоренный курс «как бесить дальнобойщиков»: внезапное перестроение из крайнего правого в левый без поворотника прямо перед капотом, экстренная остановка на автобане ради какой-то своей важной мысли и круговые развязки, живущие по законам домино. Со временем привыкаешь и даже начинаешь находить в этом извращённое удовольствие — такой тренажёр дзен-терпения и реакции. 
Поэтому к честным 8.5/10 базовой игры я бы добавил отдельно 10/10 автоподстав от ботов. Нервы доставлены вместе с грузом.

A great driving simulator, although I do wish I had a proper steering wheel setup to get the full experience. Unfortunately, I can't really justify buying all the equipment just for one game. Even so, Euro Truck Simulator 2 is still very enjoyable with a keyboard or controller.

The game runs extremely well and is well-optimized. If you ever get tired of the vanilla experience, there's an enormous selection of Steam Workshop mods that can enhance everything from graphics and trucks to gameplay mechanics and realism. The modding community is one of the game's strongest features and helps keep it fresh even after hundreds of hours.

Another great feature is TruckersMP, which allows you to drive alongside thousands of other players. Seeing real drivers on the roads, participating in convoys, and simply sharing the experience with others adds a whole new layer of immersion to the game.

Personally, driving games aren't usually my thing, which is why I tend to take long breaks from it. However, there's something incredibly relaxing about loading up the game on a quiet evening, putting on some music, and making deliveries across Europe. I can definitely see myself coming back during the winter months when I'm in the mood for a calmer gaming experience.

Overall, Euro Truck Simulator 2 is a fantastic simulator with smooth performance, endless modding possibilities, and a surprisingly relaxing gameplay loop. Even if you're not a huge fan of driving games, it's worth trying at least once.

Such a goated game. The graphics is great, the map is massive, and the trucks are highly customizable. There are plenty of mods available, allowing you to explore your creativity and tailor the game to your liking. The UI is clean and easy to use, the optimization is solid, and there are many multiplayer servers to choose from, keeping the experience fresh and enjoyable. Overall, I give it a 10/10.

Franchement, ce jeu est une pépite. J'y joue depuis environ Février 2026 et SCS Software fait un boulot de dingue avec les mises à jour régulières et les nouveaux DLC qui rendent les cartes de plus en plus réalistes.

Le gameplay est super chill : tu lances ta propre entreprise, tu achètes tes garages, tu customises tes camions et tu roules. Que ce soit pour enchaîner les kilomètres de nuit sous la pluie en écoutant la radio, ou pour admirer les paysages au coucher du soleil, l'immersion est toujours là. La physique des camions est bien foutue et la communauté de modding est juste incroyable (même si je ne joue avec aucun mod).

C’est le jeu parfait pour décompresser, et quand tu y ajoutes le multijoueur (avec TruckersMP ou les convois de base) pour rouler avec tes potes ou ta VTC, ça devient encore meilleur.

Si vous aimez la route et la simulation, foncez les yeux fermés, il vaut largement son prix ! :steerme:

Of the many things I've found in this game is that Tromso isn't permanently cold and frozen, as well as "Midnight Sun", although I wouldn't particularly want to drive there in real life.

After doing so well I ended up with 30 drivers, and trucks and essentially couldn't lose money, I started again, and made it more difficult - incrementally upping the realism settings. Oh and I found that most trucks are automatic now.

Since I live in the UK, after driving trucks in this mostly left hand drive, and driving on the right, its easy to be slightly confused when driving in reality. At first I found LHD trucks to be odd. Mastering reversing a truck in ever more difficult or tight spaces, gives an insight into real truck driving, and has given me more empathy when encountering trucks on the road.

Anyway, that's my blather out of the way. Now onto the review... or maybe just get it.

Короче, наиграл тут уже кучу времени, скупил вообще все DLC с картами (кроме Nordic Horizons, её куплю позже) и могу сказать одно - это лучший способ залипнуть и расслабиться после учёбы или тяжёлого дня.
Когда у тебя открыта вся карта, масштаб просто взрывает мозг. Берёшь какой-нибудь огромный негабаритный груз на юге Италии и тащишь его через всю Европу куда-нибудь в Питер или Финляндию. Картинка за окном меняется нереально круто: то узкие серпантины, то мелкие деревни, то огромные пустые автобаны, где можно втопить. Новые регионы разработчики вообще делают как конфету 
 - проработка городов и развязок топ.
Чисто рецепт идеального вечера: врубаешь на фоне свой плейлист с музыкой, настраиваешь зеркала, ложишься на руль и просто катишь под ночной дождь. Тюнинг грузовиков затягивает, физика приятная, едешь и ни о чём не думаешь.
Да, без DLC базовая игра выглядит старовато и скучно, но если собрать всю карту, то ETS 2 превращается в бесконечную игру, которая никогда не надоест.

10 из 10, всем советую, у кого есть свободные вечера.

Why is this game incredibly good? It's just an ordinary hauling sim... you just go around, driving from here to there... what's the point?

That is the point!

It's not just a sim; it's a sim of something you are used to, something that is a part of your daily routine (ok, you might not be a truck driver, but you are a driver, or at least a passenger). Those are the roads you see, even if you don't live in Europe, like myself, because roads are roads, cars are cars, lights are lights, and no one feels like a stranger to all of this. That's why this stupendous game is an instant click!

Other sims don't stick to us the way this one does because they simulate distant realities, like combat jets, war machines, subs, spaceships, mechs (these last two can't be sims because they simulate something that doesn't exist, but anyways...), weird jobs. And even if they give us cars, it's not to just drive quietly, but to race, bump, or run away. 

ETS2 is THE sim.

Euro Truck Simulator 2 is one of those games that sounds boring when you explain it to someone.

“You drive trucks across Europe.”

That’s it.

But somehow… it becomes one of the most peaceful, immersive, and strangely addictive gaming experiences ever made.

After nearly 200 hours, I completely understand why this game has such a loyal fanbase.

There’s something genuinely relaxing about driving through massive European highways at night while rain hits the windshield and city lights glow in the distance. The atmosphere in ETS2 is incredible. This game turns simple driving into a mood.

Every country feels different:

Germany feels organized and industrial
Scandinavia feels calm and beautiful
Italy has its own charm
Eastern Europe roads can become pure suffering 💀

The map design and environmental detail are honestly impressive for a trucking simulator. Long drives never feel completely empty because the world has personality.

And somehow this game becomes therapeutic.

After stressful competitive games, ETS2 feels like digital meditation 😌🚛

No toxic voice chat.
No battle pass pressure.
No constant explosions.

Just you, your truck, the road, and a GPS that occasionally sends you into questionable life decisions.

The progression system is surprisingly satisfying too. Starting with basic delivery jobs and slowly building your own trucking empire feels rewarding without being overwhelming. Buying new trucks, upgrading engines, customizing interiors, hiring drivers, and expanding garages gives you long-term goals while keeping the experience chill.

And the customization is fantastic.

You can create:

realistic professional trucks
glowing RGB highway monsters
or vehicles that look like they escaped a truck-themed nightclub 😂

The modding community also deserves huge respect. Real company logos, graphics overhauls, weather mods, traffic packs, sound improvements, realistic physics, map expansions — mods make an already great game even better.

What really makes ETS2 special though is the feeling it creates.

Late-night driving while listening to synthwave, ambient music, podcasts, or just the sound of the engine creates a weird emotional calm that very few games can replicate 🌃

It’s one of the only games where doing nothing but driving for an hour can actually improve your mood.

Of course, the game isn’t perfect:

AI traffic still has moments of absolute stupidity
Some deliveries become repetitive
DLC collecting can become expensive
The gameplay won’t appeal to players looking for nonstop action

But honestly, ETS2 understands exactly what it wants to be.

It’s not trying to overwhelm you.
It’s trying to give you an experience.

And somehow, driving cargo across Europe at 80 km/h becomes unforgettable.

Verdict:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I have spent a lot of hours in ETS2, but a large part of that time comes from playing on TruckersMP with friends and the community. The multiplayer experience adds a completely different level of enjoyment and keeps the game interesting even after hundreds or thousands of hours.

The game itself offers a huge map, a relaxing driving experience, and great customization options for trucks. The developers continue to update the game and add new content, which is something I appreciate.

Of course, ETS2 is not perfect. There are still some bugs, occasional AI traffic issues, physics glitches, and performance drops in certain areas. Sometimes navigation can behave strangely, and after major updates, new bugs can appear. However, none of these issues have been serious enough to stop me from enjoying the game.

Most of my playtime comes from TruckersMP rather than singleplayer, which is important to mention when looking at my total hours. Driving with real players, participating in convoys, and being part of the trucking community is what keeps me coming back.

Overall, ETS2 remains one of the best trucking simulators available and is easy to recommend to anyone who enjoys relaxing simulation games or multiplayer trucking experiences. 🚛💨

Euro Truck Simulator 2 is not just a driving game — it is one of the most immersive and relaxing simulators ever created. From the very first delivery, the game makes you feel like a real truck driver traveling across Europe. Every road, city, highway, and countryside location feels alive, detailed, and carefully designed. Whether you are driving through the rainy roads of Germany, the beautiful mountains of Austria, or the peaceful highways of Scandinavia, the atmosphere is simply incredible.

What makes ETS2 truly special is the freedom it gives to players. You are not forced into nonstop action or unrealistic gameplay. Instead, the game allows you to enjoy the journey itself. You can listen to music, watch sunsets while driving across long highways, manage your trucking company, customize your truck, and slowly build your own transport empire. It is relaxing, addictive, and surprisingly satisfying.

The graphics still look amazing even years after release. The lighting system, weather effects, and detailed truck interiors create an experience that feels realistic and immersive. Night driving during heavy rain with dashboard lights glowing inside the cabin feels unbelievably atmospheric. The developers clearly put a huge amount of passion into every detail.

Truck customization is another massive highlight. Players can modify engines, paint jobs, cabins, wheels, lights, and countless accessories to create their perfect truck. Every vehicle feels powerful and unique. Driving different trucks changes the experience completely, and learning how each one handles makes the simulator even more enjoyable.

The map expansions deserve special praise as well. DLC regions are created with incredible attention to detail, bringing new cultures, landscapes, and roads into the game. Exploring new countries never becomes boring because every area has its own personality and driving challenges.

One of the best things about Euro Truck Simulator 2 is its community. Mods make the game almost endless. Players can add realistic weather, traffic improvements, real company logos, new trucks, graphics upgrades, and even entirely new maps. Few games support creativity and community content as well as ETS2 does.

The progression system is also extremely rewarding. Starting as a small driver taking basic jobs and eventually owning a huge company with garages, hired drivers, and a fleet of trucks feels satisfying and motivating. The game constantly gives you goals without becoming stressful.

Unlike many modern games focused only on fast action, Euro Truck Simulator 2 proves that calm and immersive gameplay can be just as exciting. It is a game where the journey matters more than the destination, and that is exactly why so many players love it.

SCS Software created something timeless with ETS2. Even after hundreds of hours, the game remains relaxing, beautiful, and enjoyable. Whether you are a fan of simulators or someone simply looking for a peaceful and immersive experience, Euro Truck Simulator 2 is absolutely worth playing.

Score: 10/10 — One of the greatest simulator games ever made.
